user_query:
I’m a first-time parent and I want a simple “Pool Mode” I can run from my laptop before we head to the pool. Please create a Linux-compatible script and supporting outputs using the included files to help me reduce distractions and remember key safety steps. Here’s what I need: 

1) Implement scripts/pool_mode.sh that:
   - Reads and validates these inputs: input/config/pool_mode.yml, input/safety_checklist.csv, input/emergency_contacts.json. If any are missing, write a clear error to output/diagnostics.txt and exit.
   - Logs a brief inspection of the input directory (existence and byte sizes of those three files) to output/diagnostics.txt.
   - Checks for system tools and adjusts if possible:
     • Detect amixer (for volume), xbacklight (for brightness), and notify-send (for desktop notifications) using command -v and capture their outputs.
     • If a tool is available, attempt to set volume and brightness to the percentages in input/config/pool_mode.yml, capturing the exact stdout/stderr from each command into output/diagnostics.txt. If a tool isn’t available or a command fails, record the error output and skip that adjustment.
   - Builds a short, readable summary using input/safety_checklist.csv and input/emergency_contacts.json:
     • Include the child’s name and age from input/config/pool_mode.yml.
     • List the "before_swim" and "at_pool" steps (in that order) from the CSV. Do not include "after_swim" in the summary.
     • List emergency contacts with any preferred:true contacts first. Include each contact’s name, role, and phone.
   - If notify-send is available, send a notification showing the first three "before_swim" steps and the first preferred contact’s phone number. Whether or not notify-send is present, write the full summary text to output/last_pool_mode_summary.txt.
   - Honor the trigger file in input/config/pool_mode.yml (e.g., trigger/pool_visit.flag): if it exists, treat the run as “live Pool Mode” (attempt device adjustments as above), then remove the trigger file; if it does not exist, treat as a dry run but still produce output/last_pool_mode_summary.txt and output/diagnostics.txt.
   - Append to output/diagnostics.txt a final “Analysis” section that explains which settings were successfully applied and which were skipped, citing the observed command outputs or errors as the reason.

2) Run scripts/pool_mode.sh once to generate the outputs under output/ and ensure they are populated based on the provided inputs and your environment.

3) Draft a message to my local pool using details from input/emergency_contacts.json and input/config/pool_mode.yml:
   - Create output/messages/pool_inquiry_email.txt addressed to the local_pool.email in the JSON.
   - Include a subject line and a short, polite body that mentions my child’s name and age, and asks about: (a) lifeguard coverage and hours, (b) beginner swim lesson options for the listed age, and (c) lifejacket policy. End with my preferred contact(s) from the JSON.

Deliverables I should see:
- scripts/pool_mode.sh (executable script)
- output/last_pool_mode_summary.txt (contains the summary with before_swim and at_pool steps and prioritized contacts)
- output/diagnostics.txt (contains file checks, command detections, raw stdout/stderr from any attempted settings, and an Analysis section)
- output/messages/pool_inquiry_email.txt (email draft as specified)

Please do not rely on any internet resources. Keep everything self-contained in this workspace. The script should be idempotent — running it again with the same inputs should update the outputs consistently based on the current presence/absence of the trigger file and available system commands. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
